name: rn-testing
description: Testing patterns for React Native with Jest and React Native Testing Library. Use when writing tests, mocking Expo modules, testing Zustand stores, or debugging test failures.
capabilities: Zustand store testing with proper state reset between tests
Expo module mocking patterns for Jest
React Query component testing with QueryClientProvider
Component testing with fireEvent and waitFor
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: This skill is pure documentation containing only testing patterns for React Native. The static scanner incorrectly flagged markdown code blocks and JavaScript method names. No executable code, network calls, or file operations exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Writing tests for React Native apps requires understanding of mocking native modules, handling async state, and testing Zustand stores. This skill provides proven patterns and code examples for reliable test suites.
userTitle: Test React Native Apps with Jest
limitations: Does not execute tests or interact with test runners
Does not modify project files or Jest configuration
Does not provide mock data or test fixtures
Pattern examples are for reference; users must adapt to their codebase

useCases.title: Fix flaky store tests
useCases.description: Learn how to reset Zustand store state between tests to prevent state leakage causing intermittent test failures.

useCases.title: Mock Expo native modules
useCases.description: Set up proper mocks for expo-router, expo-secure-store, and other Expo modules in Jest tests.

useCases.title: Test async operations
useCases.description: Handle async store actions and React Query data fetching in tests using act() and waitFor.

promptTemplates.title: Basic test pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I test a React Native component with Jest and React Native Testing Library?

promptTemplates.title: Zustand store test
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I test a Zustand store and prevent state from persisting between tests?

promptTemplates.title: Expo module mock
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to mock expo-router and expo-secure-store in Jest tests.

promptTemplates.title: Async test debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: My React Native tests have act() warnings and timeouts. How do I fix them?

bestPractices: Always reset store state in beforeEach to prevent test pollution between tests
Use findBy* queries instead of getBy* for async content to avoid race conditions
Mock external dependencies like expo-router at the module level for consistent behavior
antiPatterns: Using getBy* queries for async content that may not appear immediately
Forgetting to clear mocks with jest.clearAllMocks() between navigation tests
Testing implementation details instead of user-visible behavior

faq.question: Which testing frameworks does this skill cover?
faq.answer: Jest and React Native Testing Library are the primary frameworks. Patterns work with Expo and non-Expo React Native projects.

faq.question: What Jest version is required?
faq.answer: Patterns work with Jest 29+ which is standard in modern React Native projects using Expo 49 or higher.

faq.question: How do I integrate with my existing test setup?
faq.answer: Copy pattern examples directly into your test files. Mock configurations can be added to jest.setup.js for global availability.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my project files?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill that provides guidance. You manually implement patterns in your codebase.

faq.question: Why do I see act() warnings in my tests?
faq.answer: State updates occur outside act() wrapper. Use act() for async operations and waitFor() for assertions on async content.

faq.question: How is this different from rn-zustand-patterns skill?
faq.answer: rn-zustand-patterns covers implementing stores. This skill focuses specifically on testing stores, components, and hooks that use them.
